option.name: filePath
option.value: /GoogleSheetsResultWriter (snapshot).java

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
filePath
option.value: /api-test (snapshot).yml

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
name: API Tests
on: push: branches: [ master ] pull_request: branches: [ master ] workflow_dispatch: repository_dispatch: types: [run-google-sheet-test]
jobs: api-tests: runs-on: ubuntu-latest env: OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }} FIREBASE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_API_KEY }} FIREBASE_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_EMAIL }} FIREBASE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PASSWORD }} CMS_BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CMS_BASE_URL }} GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_JSON: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_JSON }}
steps: - name: Checkout uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Set up JDK 17 and cache Maven uses: actions/setup-java@v4 with: java-version: '17' distribution: 'temurin' cache: 'maven'
- name: Build and run tests id: run_tests run: mvn -B -V -e -DskipTests=false test
- name: Upload test reports if: always() uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: test-reports path: target/surefire-reports
- name: Update Google Sheet on success if: success() && github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' run: | curl -X POST "${{ secrets.APPS_SCRIPT_WEBAPP_URL }}" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "spreadsheetId": "${{ github.event.client_payload.spreadsheetId }}", "sheetName": "${{ github.event.client_payload.sheetName }}", "runCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.runCell }}", "statusCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.statusCell }}", "lastRunCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.lastRunCell }}", "messageCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.messageCell }}", "status": "PASSED", "message": "Automation test completed successfully." }'
- name: Update Google Sheet on failure if: failure() && github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' run: | curl -X POST "${{ secrets.APPS_SCRIPT_WEBAPP_URL }}" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "spreadsheetId": "${{ github.event.client_payload.spreadsheetId }}", "sheetName": "${{ github.event.client_payload.sheetName }}", "runCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.runCell }}", "statusCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.statusCell }}", "lastRunCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.lastRunCell }}", "messageCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.messageCell }}", "status": "FAILED", "message": "Automation test failed." }'

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
name: API Tests
on: push: branches: [ master ] pull_request: branches: [ master ] workflow_dispatch: repository_dispatch: types: [run-google-sheet-test]
jobs: api-tests: runs-on: ubuntu-latest env: OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }} FIREBASE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_API_KEY }} FIREBASE_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_EMAIL }} FIREBASE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.FIREBASE_PASSWORD }} CMS_BASE_URL: ${{ secrets.CMS_BASE_URL }}
steps: - name: Checkout uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Set up Google credentials if: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_JSON != '' }} run: | echo '${{ secrets.GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_JSON }}' > $HOME/google-credentials.json echo "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=$HOME/google-credentials.json" >> $GITHUB_ENV echo "Google credentials file created at $HOME/google-credentials.json"
- name: Set up JDK 17 and cache Maven uses: actions/setup-java@v4 with: java-version: '17' distribution: 'temurin' cache: 'maven'
- name: Build and run tests id: run_tests run: mvn -B -V -e -DskipTests=false test
- name: Upload test reports if: always() uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: test-reports path: target/surefire-reports
- name: Clean up credentials if: always() run: | rm -f $HOME/google-credentials.json || true
- name: Update Google Sheet on success if: success() && github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' run: | curl -X POST "${{ secrets.APPS_SCRIPT_WEBAPP_URL }}" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "spreadsheetId": "${{ github.event.client_payload.spreadsheetId }}", "sheetName": "${{ github.event.client_payload.sheetName }}", "runCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.runCell }}", "statusCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.statusCell }}", "lastRunCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.lastRunCell }}", "messageCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.messageCell }}", "status": "PASSED", "message": "Automation test completed successfully." }'
- name: Update Google Sheet on failure if: failure() && github.event_name == 'repository_dispatch' run: | curl -X POST "${{ secrets.APPS_SCRIPT_WEBAPP_URL }}" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "spreadsheetId": "${{ github.event.client_payload.spreadsheetId }}", "sheetName": "${{ github.event.client_payload.sheetName }}", "runCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.runCell }}", "statusCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.statusCell }}", "lastRunCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.lastRunCell }}", "messageCell": "${{ github.event.client_payload.messageCell }}", "status": "FAILED", "message": "Automation test failed." }'

option.name: filePath
option.value: /chat-google-sheets-validation (snapshot).feature

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
filePath
option.value: /intent-evaluator-system.prompt (snapshot).txt

option.name: originalContent
option.value:
You are an Intent Classification Validator.
Your job is to evaluate whether the AI assistant's intent classification response CORRECTLY FOLLOWS its given instructions (LLMRequestFormattedPrompt), NOT to judge the user's message.
CRITICAL RULES: - DO NOT judge or evaluate the user's message - it can be anything - ONLY validate if the LLM response correctly follows the classification rules in the prompt - The LLM should classify according to the available choices in the prompt - If a valid intent from the choices is returned, verify it makes sense given the prompt logic
The validation must check: 1. Did the LLM correctly apply the classification rules from its prompt? 2. Is the output in the correct format as specified? 3. Is the returned intent one of the valid choices defined in the prompt? 4. Did the LLM follow the classification logic defined in its instructions?
You must detect: - Hallucinated intents (intents not in the available choices) - Incorrect format responses - Clear misclassification based on the prompt's own rules
You MUST return only valid JSON. No markdown. No explanations outside JSON.

option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
You are an Intent Classification Validator.
Your job is to evaluate whether the AI assistant's intent classification response CORRECTLY FOLLOWS its given instructions (LLMRequestFormattedPrompt), NOT to judge the user's message.
CRITICAL RULES: - DO NOT judge or evaluate the user's message - it can be anything - ONLY validate if the LLM response correctly follows the classification rules in the prompt - The LLM should classify according to the available choices in the prompt - If a valid intent from the choices list is returned, that is a VALID response - DO NOT second-guess the classification choice - You do NOT have ground truth. You CANNOT determine whether the LLM picked the "correct" intent - only flag provably wrong things
The validation must check: 1. Is the output in the correct format as specified? 2. Is the returned intent one of the valid choices defined in the prompt? (if not, that is a failure) 3. Did the LLM follow the format and structural rules defined in its instructions?
You must detect ONLY: - Hallucinated intents (intents NOT in the available choices list provided in the prompt) - Incorrect format responses - Clear format or structural violations of the prompt rules
You MUST NOT: - Decide which intent "should" have been chosen - Fail the response because you would have classified it differently
You MUST return only valid JSON. No markdown. No explanations outside JSON.

option.name: filePath
option.value: /intent-evaluator-user.prompt (snapshot).txt

option.name: originalContent
updatedContent
filePath
option.value: /pgf-utils (snapshot).js

filePath
option.value: /specificquestionsubintent-evaluator-user.prompt (snapshot).txt
